fact_nuggets.text: Pickup (Japanese: Pickup) is an Ability introduced in Generation III.

Description [ edit | edit source ] Games Description MD R B Finds items if the Pokemon is not holding anything. MD T D S Enables the Pokemon to find items occasionally when it is not holding anything. B S L MD GtI The Pokemon may pick up an item when it goes to a different floor. * When the Pokemon goes to a different floor, it may pick up an item! But if it is already holding an item, it won't pick up anything. * S MD When the Pokemon goes to a different floor, it may pick up an item! But if it is already holding an item, it won't pick up anything. MD RT DX When the Pokemon goes to a different floor, it may pick up an item. However, there will be places where there is nothing for the Pokemon to pick up.

Contents 1 Effect 1.1 In battle 1.1.1 Generations III and IV 1.1.2 Generation V-VIII 1.1.3 Generation IX 1.2 Outside of battle 1.2.1 Items received 1.2.1.1 Pokemon Ruby, Sapphire, and Colosseum 1.2.1.2 Pokemon FireRed and LeafGreen 1.2.1.3 Pokemon Emerald 1.2.1.3.1 Battle Pyramid 1.2.1.4 Pokemon XD: Gale of Darkness 1.2.1.5 Pokemon Diamond, Pearl, and Platinum 1.2.1.6 Pokemon HeartGold and SoulSilver 1.2.1.7 Pokemon Black, White, Black 2, White 2, X, and Y 1.2.1.8 Pokemon Omega Ruby and Alpha Sapphire 1.2.1.9 Pokemon Sun, Moon, Ultra Sun, and Ultra Moon 1.2.1.10 Pokemon Sword and Shield 1.2.1.11 Pokemon Brilliant Diamond and Shining Pearl 1.2.1.12 Pokemon Scarlet and Violet Effect [ edit | edit source ] In battle [ edit | edit source ] Generations III and IV [ edit | edit source ] Pickup has no effect during battle. Generation V-VIII [ edit | edit source ] At the end of the turn in which another Pokemon used a one-time-use item (including throwing an item with Fling or using Natural Gift ), a Pokemon with Pickup will collect the item if they are not holding anything. If more than one Pokemon has Pickup or Harvest , the faster Pokemon will restore an item first. If multiple items can be collected, the Pokemon will collect the item most recently consumed. A single Pokemon's Pickup will not activate more than once per turn. Pickup cannot collect an item if the Pokemon that consumed the item is no longer on the field at the end of the turn, or if the item has already been recovered (such as with Recycle , another Pokemon's Pickup, or Harvest). Pickup cannot collect an item that a Pokemon consumed before switching out, even if it switched back in again before the end of the turn. Pickup cannot collect items that have been destroyed with Incinerate , eaten with Bug Bite or Pluck , or knocked off with Knock Off . A popped Air Balloon cannot be collected. A Pokemon may only have one consumed item at a time; if a Pokemon consumes an item, then obtains another item and consumes it, the original consumed item cannot be collected, even if the second item is collected or restored then swapped away. If the Pokemon obtains another item after consuming an item, Pickup can still collect the old item as long as the Pokemon does not consume the new one. Generation IX [ edit | edit source ] A Pokemon with Pickup recovers its consumed held item at the end of the turn in a battle against a wild Pokemon . Outside of battle [ edit | edit source ] This section is incomplete. Please feel free to edit this section to add missing information and complete it. Reason: in which games can pickup activate when you catch a Pokemon? After winning a battle, any Pokemon in the player's party that has this Ability and is not already holding an item has a 10% chance of creating a held item for itself, even if it did not participate in the battle and/or cannot battle . In Generation VIII , Pokemon with this Ability can find items even in the Battle Tower . Items received [ edit | edit source ] Pokemon Ruby, Sapphire, and Colosseum [ edit | edit source ] Rarity 30% 10% 5% 4% 1% Item Pokemon FireRed and LeafGreen [ edit | edit source ] In Pokemon FireRed and LeafGreen , the Ability focuses on Berries , which are not as readily available in Kanto and the Sevii Islands as they are in Hoenn . Rarity 15% 10% 5% 1% Item Pokemon Emerald [ edit | edit source ] In Pokemon Emerald , the Ability was changed to be affected by the Ability user's level. Level 30% 10% 4% 1% 1-10 11-20 21-30 31-40 41-50 51-60 61-70 71-80 81-90 91-100 Battle Pyramid [ edit | edit source ] In addition, the Battle Pyramid has a separate list of items that can be received; this table, however, is instead based on the number of floors/rounds the player has completed inside the Pyramid. The Pickup table and the item balls on the ground share this same pool of items. Picks up other Pokemon's used and Flung held items. May also pick up an item after battle.
